entities.description: A table containing 6 years of transaction data for a single account, including details like transaction date, description, merchant, category, and amounts.

columns.name: Account
columns.business_meaning: Unique identifier for the account associated with the transactions.

columns.name: Value Date
columns.business_meaning: Date when the transaction value was posted to the account.

columns.name: Ref/Cheque No.
columns.business_meaning: Reference or cheque number associated with the transaction.

columns.name: Merchant
columns.business_meaning: Name of the merchant or entity involved in the transaction.

columns.name: MCC
columns.business_meaning: Merchant Category Code, a 4-digit number representing the merchant's business category.

columns.name: Category
columns.business_meaning: Broad category of the transaction (e.g., Income, Housing, Utilities).

columns.name: Channel
columns.business_meaning: Channel through which the transaction was conducted (e.g., NEFT, POS, NET).

columns.name: Debit
columns.business_meaning: Amount debited from the account in the transaction.

columns.name: Credit
columns.business_meaning: Amount credited to the account in the transaction.

instant_insights.biggest_driver: The largest contributor to expenses is housing, with a single rent payment of ₹23,000 dominating the dataset.
instant_insights.key_trend: Recurring grocery and fuel expenses are evident, with multiple transactions at BigBasket and fuel stations throughout the month.
instant_insights.top_insight: The dataset reflects a balanced mix of income and essential expenses, with a focus on housing, utilities, groceries, and transport, indicating a typical monthly financial pattern.
